foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Understanding fluid compartments and volume status is essential for deciding when crystalloids are insufficient and blood products are needed

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Blood transfusion restores oxygen-carrying capacity and coagulation factors disrupted by hemorrhage; understanding normal hemostatic mechanisms guides appropriate component therapy

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Hemorrhagic shock (class III/IV) requires massive transfusion protocols with packed RBCs, FFP, and platelets in defined ratios

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Perioperative blood loss estimation and transfusion triggers (Hb <7-8 g/dL) are core to safe surgical management

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Major burns cause hemoconcentration initially then anemia from hemolysis and marrow suppression, requiring staged transfusion support

objectives: SU3.1: Describe the Indications and appropriate use of blood and blood
SU3.3: Counsel patients and family/ friends for blood transfusion and blood A/C
integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Blood Transfusion across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
